Usage: pan [options] [command]

Multi-agent orchestration for AI coding assistants

Options:
  -V, --version                   output the version number
  --yolo [value]                  Override permission mode for spawned Claude
                                  Code agents. Default is auto (Claude Code's
                                  classifier blocks destructive ops). --yolo or
                                  --yolo=true switches to
                                  --dangerously-skip-permissions; --yolo=false
                                  (--no-yolo) forces auto mode. Equivalent to
                                  setting PAN_YOLO=true|false. Works in any
                                  argv position. Falls back to
                                  config.claude.permissionMode.
  -h, --help                      display help for command

Commands:
  init                            Initialize Panopticon (~/.panopticon/)
  sync [options]                  Sync skills/agents to ~/.claude/ and render
                                  the context layers
  context                         Manage the layered context model (global /
                                  project / workspace)
  restore [timestamp]             Restore from backup
  backup                          Manage backups
  skills [options]                List and manage skills
  issues [options]                List and triage work across configured
                                  trackers
  show [options] <id>             Unified lens: shadow state, CV, context,
                                  health for one issue
  open [options] <id>             Open an issue workspace in your preferred
                                  editor
  review                          Review-loop management: pending items,
                                  request re-review, reset cycles
  plan [options] [id]             Planning lifecycle commands
  tell <id> <message>             Send message to running agent
  kill [options] <id>             Stop running agent (workspace preserved)
  pause [options] <id>            Persistently pause an agent and stop it if
                                  running
  unpause <id>                    Clear an agent pause gate without spawning it
  untroubled <id>                 Clear an agent troubled gate without spawning
                                  it
  fork [options] <conv>           Summary Fork a conversation — creates new
                                  session from a summary of previous work
  unarchive-conversation <query>  Restore an archived conversation by exact
                                  name or matching title
  resume [options] <id>           Resume from saved Claude session
  recover [options] [id]          Recover crashed or stopped agent
  sync-main <id>                  Merge latest main into workspace feature
                                  branch
  done [options] <id>             Mark work complete, move to review
  approve <id>                    [REMOVED] Use dashboard MERGE button instead
  reopen [options] <id>           Re-enter the pipeline for a
                                  closed/completed/cancelled issue (resets
                                  specialist state). For issues already in
                                  progress, use `pan review restart`.
  wipe [options] <id>             Destructive: removes workspace files, kills
                                  processes, deletes branches, clears review
                                  state, and resets tracker status
  destroy [options] <id>          Alias for workspace destroy: remove the issue
                                  workspace worktree and branch
  close [options] <id>            Verify, clean up, and close issue on tracker
  start [options] <id>            Create workspace and spawn agent for an issue
  swarm [options] [id]            Swarm execution: spawn parallel agents across
                                  vBRIEF plan items using dependency-wave
                                  scheduling
  workspace                       Workspace management
  test                            Test running and management
  tts                             Local TTS daemon helpers
  release                         Intentional release flow for stable and
                                  canary publishes
  memory                          Search and inspect Panopticon memory
  admin                           Plumbing commands: watchdog, specialists,
                                  infra, db, config, and more
  conversations|conv              Discover, index, and search Claude Code
                                  session history
  install [options]               Install Panopticon prerequisites
  inspect [options] <issueId>     Request inspection of a completed bead before
                                  proceeding to the next
  caveman-compress <file>         Compress a static reference doc using
                                  caveman-compress (manual use only, never
                                  automated). Calls the Claude API recursively
                                  to compress prose by ~65-75% while preserving
                                  technical accuracy.
  scope                           vBRIEF lifecycle management
  flywheel                        Flywheel orchestrator lifecycle and status
                                  helpers
  status [options]                Show running agents
  dev [options]                   Start dashboard in development mode with Vite
                                  HMR
  up [options]                    Start dashboard (and Traefik if enabled)
  down [options]                  Stop dashboard (and Traefik if enabled)
  reload [options]                Build Panopticon, then restart the dashboard
                                  only after the build succeeds
  restart [options]               Restart a platform component (default:
                                  dashboard only — leaves CLIProxy, Traefik,
                                  TLDR running)
  project                         Project registry for multi-project workspace
                                  support
  health                          Show runtime health of Panopticon services
  doctor [options]                Check system health and dependencies
  resources [options]             Show RAM usage by agents, conversations, and
                                  system processes
  update [options]                Update Panopticon to latest version
  cost                            Track and report AI usage costs
  serve [options]                 Start the dashboard server and open it in the
                                  default browser (npx launcher)
  help [command]                  display help for command
